title: Gen AI · Talk to the Model
subtitle: Branch 1 — credentials, client, one invoke (provider-agnostic)

nodes.label: Env & credentials
nodes.definition: Virtual env + secrets + model id + embedding model id. Every provider needs these three ideas: who am I calling, which LLM, which embedder.
nodes.whyItMatters: Generic pattern — works for OpenAI, Anthropic, IBM, Azure, local Ollama. Names change; shape stays.

note.summary:
Typical env vars (names vary by provider):
• API key / token
• LLM model id
• Embedding model id

nodes.label: LLM client factory
nodes.definition: One small module that returns a chat model and an embeddings client — LangChain and scripts import the same factory.
nodes.whyItMatters: Swap provider in one place; labs keep the same import names.
nodes.safeSentence: One factory; many scripts share it.

nodes.label: Swap model id
nodes.definition: Same invoke, different model id — compare wording and style, not retrieval.

nodes.label: Smoke invoke
nodes.definition: One successful text generation before you build chains, RAG, or UI.

nodes.label: Example · IBM watsonx
nodes.definition: Coursera labs often use watsonx.ai — Granite, Mistral, project id, region URL.
nodes.safeSentence: One provider option you practiced on IBM track.

nodes.label: Example · OpenAI API
nodes.definition: ChatOpenAI + OpenAIEmbeddings — common local/dev path; same LangChain pipe shape.
nodes.safeSentence: API key + model name — widely used pattern.

paths.label: First invoke lane
paths.description: Credentials → client factory → one successful call. Provider links are optional examples.
